request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate a batch of extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We must accept only items that are "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge." Reject items that are legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

We need to output a JSON object with a "verdicts" array, one per candidate. Each verdict has candidate_id, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge, reason, and category_if_not (null if accepted).

Let's review each candidate:

cand_862d844a3b1d3c49: "Plant Enable Logic" - Summary: Enable the plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes and conditions are met. Evidence quote: "5.20.2.2. Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:" This seems to be a direct operational sequence from the guideline. It is useful and grounded. Accept.

cand_ea7bfef5946364fd: "Plant Disable Logic" - Summary: Disable the plant when enabled for at least 15 minutes and any condition is met. Evidence quote: "Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive." This is a direct operational rule. Accept.

cand_0d251412698de27f: "WSE Enable Logic" - Summary: Enable waterside economizer if disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX > PHXLWT + 2°F. Evidence quote supports it. Accept.

cand_6898e1b1fceb759c: "WSE Disable Logic" - Summary: Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX > CHWRT upstream of HX less 1°F for 2 minutes. Evidence quote: "Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes (i.e., if the HX is not reducing the CHW temp by at least 1°F)." This is direct. Accept.

cand_5b187e627e1a6a71: "PHXLWT Tuning - Decrease m" - Summary: Decrease 'm' by 0.02 when economizer is disabled if it remained enabled for greater than 60 minutes. Evidence: "Decrease “m” by 0.02 when the economizer is disabled if the economizer remained enabled for greater than 60 minutes." Accept.

cand_c31ee5f25bcfe85b: "PHXLWT Tuning - Increase m" - Summary: Increase 'm' by 0.02 when economizer is disabled if it remained enabled for less than 30 minutes and WseTower-MaxSpeed did not decrease below 100%. Evidence: "Increase “m” by 0.02 when the economizer is disabled if the economizer remained enabled for less than 30 minutes and WseTower-MaxSpeed did not decrease below 100% speed while the WSE was enabled." Accept.

cand_98886caaf89322a4: "WSE HX Bypass Valve Modulation" - Summary: Modulate WSE in-line CHW return line valve to maintain DP across CHW side of HX at HX DP-Design. Evidence: "5.20.3.5. When the WSE in -line CHW return line valve is enabled, it shall be modulated by a direct -acting" The evidence quote is cut off, but it seems to be a genuine sequence. The summary is about maintaining DP. It is an operational sequence. Accept.

cand_57f788b8acaaab27: "WSE HX Pump Speed Reset Requests" - Summary: Generate WSE HX Pump Speed Reset Requests based on ΔT between CHWRT upstream of WSE and WSE HX entering CHW temperature. Evidence: "WSE HX Pump Speed Reset Requests shall be generated based on the difference (ΔT) between chilled water return temperature upstream of the WSE and WSE HX entering CHW temperature. a. If ΔT exceeds 2°F, send 2 requests until ΔT is less than 1.2°F. b. Else if ΔT exceeds 1°F, send 1 request until ΔT is less than 0.2°F. c. Else send 0 requests." This is a clear operational rule. Accept.

cand_7cb7eb622902c673: "WSE HX Pump Speed Trim & Respond" - Summary: Reset WSE HX pump speed using Trim & Respond logic with specified parameters. Evidence: "5.20.3.9. When the WSE HX pump is proven on, WSE HX pump speed shall be reset using Trim & Respond" The evidence is truncated, but it's a direct reference from the guideline. Accept.

cand_2700ae21430b7173: "Chiller Stage Minimum Runtime" - Summary: Each chiller stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes. Evidence: "Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes." Direct and useful. Accept.

cand_21ba6bbd27b0809e: "Stage Up Efficiency Condition (Primary-Only without WSE)" - Summary: Stage up if current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and OPLR not decreasing > 2.5%/min averaged over 5 minutes. Evidence: "Efficiency Condition: Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; or" Accept.

cand_082a912f012cd93f: "Stage Down Condition (Primary-Only without WSE)" - Summary: Stage down if next lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and not increasing > 2.5%/min, and failsafe condition not true. Evidence: "Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true." Accept.

cand_e1a23a16f34e176b: "Stage Up Efficiency Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)" - Similar to previous. Accept.

cand_6a49938ede3dc102: "Stage Up Failsafe Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)" - Summary: Stage up if secondary CHW supply temp > primary CHW supply temp + 2°F for 10 minutes and primary pumps at max speed, or primary CHW supply temp > setpoint by 2°F for 15 minutes. Evidence: "Failsafe Condition: i. Secondary CHW supply temperature > primary CHW supply temperature + 2°F for 10 minutes and the enabled primary pumps are at maximum speed; or ii. Primary CHW supply temperature is 2°F > setpoint for 15 minutes." Accept.

cand_badf69ed4ed082b1: "Stage Down Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)" - Similar to previous. Accept.

cand_297979d1d58ac455: "CHW Plant Reset - DP and CHWST Mapping" - Summary: Reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax from 0% to 50% loop output, then reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin from 50% to 100% loop output. Evidence: "a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin." This is a clear mapping. Accept.

cand_1759c8e703882cc2: "CHW Plant Reset Trim & Respond Parameters" - Summary: Reset CHW Plant Reset variable using Trim & Respond logic with specified parameters. Evidence: "CHW Plant Reset variable shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the following parameters: Variable Value Device Any CHW Pump Distribution Loop SP0 100% SPmin 0% SPmax 100% Td 15 minutes T 5 minutes I 2 R Cooling CHWST Reset Requests SPtrim -2% SPres +3% SPres-max +7%" This is a table of parameters. Accept.

cand_1b914130526c84c7: "Primary CHW Pump Enable/Disable (Parallel, Headered, No WSE)" - Summary: Enable lead primary CHW pump when any chiller CHW isolation valve is commanded open; disable when all are commanded closed. Evidence: "Enable lead primary CHW pump when any chiller CHW isolation valve is commanded open. Disable the lead primary CHW pump when all chiller CHW isolation valves are commanded closed." Accept.

cand_22a2741295072fa4: "Primary CHW Pump Enable/Disable (Parallel, Headered, with WSE)" - Summary: Enable lead primary CHW pump when any chiller CHW isolation valve is commanded open or WSE is enabled; disable when all chiller CHW isolation valves are commanded closed and WSE is disabled. Evidence: "Enable lead primary CHW pump when any chiller CHW isolation valve is commanded open or WSE is enabled. Disable the lead primary CHW pump when all chiller CHW isolation valves are commanded closed and WSE is disabled." Accept.

cand_60cac17b81fb908e: "Coil Pump Enable/Disable (Good Turndown)" - Summary: Enable coil pump(s) when plant enabled and pump speed command > 30% for 5 minutes; disable when plant disabled or pump speed command at minimum for 5 minutes. Evidence: "Enable the coil pump(s) when plant is enabled and the coil pump speed command exceeds 30% for 5 minutes continuously. Disable the pump(s) when either the plant is disabled or the pump speed command drops to minimum speed for 5 minutes continuously." Accept.

cand_668abfbed93162ca: "Chilled Water Reset Requests from Coil Pumps" - Summary: Send reset requests based on pump speed and supply air temperature conditions. Evidence: "1. If any coil pump is proven on, pump speed exceeds 99% for 2 minutes, and the supply air temperature exceeds the supply air temperature setpoint by 5°F for 2 minutes, send 3 Requests, 2. Else if any coil pump is proven on, pump speed exceeds 99% for 2 minutes, and the supply air temperature exceeds the supply air temperature setpoint by 3°F for 2 minutes, send 2 Requests, 3. Else if any coil pump is proven on and pump speed exceeds 95%, send 1 Request until pump speed is less than 85% or no coil pumps serving the coil are proven on. 4. Else if the coil pump speed is less than 95%, send 0 Requests." This is detailed. Accept.

cand_414742089bb82338: "Minimum Flow Bypass Valve Setpoint (Parallel Chillers)" - Summary: Modulate bypass valve to maintain minimum flow setpoint equal to MinFlowRatio times sum of CHW-DesFlowX for operating chillers. Evidence: "Bypass valve shall modulate to maintain minimum flow as measured by the chilled water flow meter at a setpoint that provides minimum flow through all operating chillers, determined as follows: a. For the chillers operating in the stage, identify the chiller with the highest ratio, MinFlowRatio, of CHW-MinFlowX to CHW-DesFlowX. b. Calculate the minimum flow setpoint as MinFlowRatio multiplied by the sum of CHW-DesFlowX for the operating chillers." Accept.

cand_e3a99489aee5c21d: "Minimum Flow Bypass Valve Setpoint (Series Chillers)" - Summary: Modulate bypass valve to maintain minimum flow setpoint equal to largest CHW-MinFlowX of operating series chillers. Evidence: "Bypass valve shall modulate to maintain minimum flow as measured by the chilled water flow meter at a setpoint equal to the largest CHW-MinFlowX of the operating series chillers." Accept.

cand_955e355e9fe2b701: "Condenser Water Pump Enable/Disable (Headered, with WSE)" - Evidence: "5.20.9.2. Enable lead CW pump when any chiller or WSE CW isolation valve is co mmanded open." Cut off, but seems plausible. Accept.

cand_cb20f5a7ce3a013f: "Condenser Water Pump Enable/Disable (Headered, No WSE)" - Evidence: "Enable lead CW pump when any chiller CW isolation valve is commanded open. Disable the lead CW pump when all chiller CW isolation valves are commanded closed." Accept.

cand_9240d68aa227ba06: "Condenser Water Pump Enable/Disable (Dedicated)" - Evidence: "5.20.9.4. Enable lea d CW pump when the lead chiller is required to run, but prior to the chiller being" Cut off, but it's a section heading. The summary says "Enable lead CW pump when lead chiller is required to run, prior to chiller enable; disable when lead chiller is disabled and either proven off for 3 minutes or not requesting CW flow." That seems plausible from context. Accept.

cand_3c26d8925e5b2dae: "Head Pressure Control Loop (No Chiller Output)" - Summary: If head pressure control signal not available from chiller, use reverse acting PID loop to maintain CWRT - CHWST at LIFTminX. Evidence: "Otherwise, if a head pressure control signal is not available from the chiller controller, a reverse acting PID loop shall maintain the temperature differential between the chiller’s condenser water return temperature and chilled water supply temperature at LIFTminX." Accept.

cand_b2004347dd620e9d: "Head Pressure Control Mapping (Fixed Speed CW Pumps)" - Summary: Map loop output: 0-50% resets HpTowerMaxSpd from 100% to minimum speed; 50-100% resets head pressure control valve from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos. Evidence: "a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos." Accept.

cand_9393b30c27020665: "Head Pressure Control Mapping (Variable Speed CW Pumps, No WSE)" - Summary: Map loop output: 0-50% resets HpTowerMaxSpd from 100% to minimum speed; 50-100% resets CW pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed. Evidence: "a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed." Accept.

cand_d20793e3b0d27656: "Head Pressure Control Mapping (WSE Enabled)" - Summary: When WSE enabled, HpTowerMaxSpeed = 100%, CW pump speed = design speed, and head pressure control loop resets valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos. Evidence: "a. Maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpeed, shall be 100% irrespective of loop output. b. Condenser water pump speed shall be equal to the design speed for the stage irrespective of loop output. c. Each enabled chiller’s head pressure control loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open at 0% loop output to MinCWVlvPos at 100% loop output." Accept.

cand_ac306e36feaf679d: "Water Treatment Override" - Summary: Every night at 1:00 am, if all CW pumps off and less than 20 minutes runtime in last 36 hours, open all isolation valves, start lead CW pump, run for 20 minutes then release. Evidence: "5.20.11.1. Every night at 1:00 am, if all condenser water pumps are off and the condenser water pumps have" Cut off, but the summary likely matches the full rule. Accept.

cand_29b376422b404f28: "Tower Staging - Quantity Map" - Summary: Quantity of enabled cooling tower cells maps to plant stage per project-specific table. Evidence: "b. Quantity of enabled cooling tower cells shall map to Plant Stage. Plant Stage Enabled Tower Cells 0 0 0 + WSE 2 1 2 1 + WSE 4 2 4 2 + WSE 4" Accept.

cand_9ed02d77ea0c8d34: "CWRT Control - CWRT Setpoint Calculation" - Summary: Calculate CWRT setpoint as CHWSTsp + LIFTtarget, where LIFTtarget = Max(LIFTmin, Min(LIFTmax, A*PLRplant + B)). Evidence: "If controlling cooling towers to maintain CWST (see 5.20.12.2 ), CWST sensor must be hardwired to the same" The evidence quote seems mismatched; it's about CWST sensor wiring, not CWRT setpoint calculation. Possibly the evidence quote is wrong. The summary might be from another section. This could be a case of unsupported summary. Let's check: The candidate title is "CWRT Control - CWRT Setpoint Calculation", but evidence mentions CWST. The summary describes a formula. The evidence quote is not supporting the summary directly. That is suspicious. We should reject if the evidence does not support the summary. The evidence quote is from page 39, which might be a different context. Could be a mistake. I'll mark as invalid with category "unsupported".

cand_913d1fd1cb327a84: "CWRT Control - Tower Speed Command (Close Coupled)" - Summary: Tower speed command is the lowest of CWRTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd from each chiller, and PlrTowerMaxSpd. Evidence: same mismatched evidence. Reject as unsupported.

cand_727f55ac5493c1d2: "CWRT Control - Tower Speed Command (Not Close Coupled)" - Summary: Control CWST to setpoint derived from CWRTsp minus CWdt; tower speed command is lowest of CWSTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd, and PlrTowerMaxSpd. Evidence: same mismatched. Reject.

cand_4dad4d8049ca064b: "Tower Fan Disable Logic (CWRT Control)" - Summary: Disable tower fans if any enabled chiller's HpTowerMaxSpd at minimum for 5 minutes, or fans at minimum for 5 minutes and CWRT/CWST below setpoint minus 1°F. Evidence: same mismatched. Reject.

cand_66bdb2c3dfdbc6b1: "Tower Fan Enable Logic (CWRT Control)" - Summary: Enable tower fans if off for at least 1 minute, CWRT/CWST rises above setpoint by 1°F, and all enabled chillers' HpTowerMaxSpd > minimum speed. Evidence: same mismatched. Reject.

cand_ad42dd75fd6682cc: "CWST Control - Initial Setpoint" - Summary: When plant first enabled, initialize CWST setpoint to 10°F less than CWSTdes. Evidence: "When the plant is first enabled, initialize CWST setpoint, CWSTsp, to 10°F less than CWSTdes." This is direct. Accept.

cand_a809a412fb9e6aef: "CWST Control - Stepwise Reset Logic" - Summary: At end of each time interval, reset CWSTsp down 1°F if conditions allow, else up 1°F, else no change. Evidence: again mismatched ("If controlling cooling towers to maintain CWST..."). Reject as unsupported.

cand_442c3ae25d87e061: "WSE Mode - Integrated Operation Fan Speed" - Summary: When WSE enabled and chiller(s) running, fan speed = WseTower-MaxSpeed, reset by PID loop maintaining chiller load at 110% of sum of MinUnloadCapX. Evidence: same mismatched. Reject.

cand_e270aa1a991fc88d: "WSE Mode - WSE Only Fan Control" - Summary: When WSE running alone, modulate fan speed to maintain CHWST at setpoint; cycle fans off if CHWST drops below setpoint and fans at minimum for 5 minutes. Evidence: same mismatched. Reject.

cand_4710abca96307a20: "Tower Make-up Water Valve Control" - Summary: Cycle make-up water valve based on tower water fill level sensor. Evidence: "Make-up water valve shall cycle based on tower water fill level sensor. The valve shall open when water level falls below T-level-min-fill. It shall close when the water level goes above T-level-max-fill." Accept.

cand_c0af181819e40df3: "Emergency Chiller Off (with WSE)" - Summary: Lock off chillers upon emergency switch; index remaining equipment to Stage 0+WSE until plant disabled or switch released. Evidence: "5.20.14.1. Chillers shall be locked off (start/stop points overridden to off at highest protocol priority) upon" Cut off, but plausible. Accept.

cand_6e6a2b36e8d1b387: "Emergency Chiller Off (without WSE)" - Summary: Lock off chillers upon emergency switch; after 5 minutes, shut off all pumps and towers. Evidence: "Chillers shall be locked off (start/stop points overridden to off at highest protocol priority) upon closing of emergency chiller off switch located at chiller room entry. After 5 minutes, shut off all pumps and towers." Accept.

cand_cdeb9fccc25effe5: "Piping Heat Trace Control" - Summary: Enable heat trace when outdoor air temperature drops below 34°F; disable when rises above 40°F. Evidence: "a. Enable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature drops below 34°F. b. Disable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature rises above 40°F." Accept.

cand_57c00a9948618d61: "Pump Maintenance Interval Alarm" - Summary: Generate Level 4 alarm when pump has operated for more than 3000 hours. Evidence: "Maintenance interval alarm when pump has operated for more than 3000 hours as indicated by the Staging Runtime: Level 4." Accept.

cand_2297c13ad31fbe6a: "Chiller Maintenance Interval Alarm" - Generate Level 4 alarm when chiller has operated for more than 1000 hours. Evidence: "Maintenance interval alarm when chiller has operated for more than 1000 hours as indicated by the Staging Runtime: Level 4." Accept.

cand_6d8313c335cbbd67: "Tower Water Level Alarms" - Level 2 if below T-level-low-alarm, Level 3 if above T-level-high-alarm. Evidence: "a. If tower water level sensor indicates water level below T-level-low-alarm, generate a Level 2 alarm. b. If tower water level sensor indicates water level above T-level-high-alarm, generate a Level 3 alarm." Accept.

cand_7dad6da47a4e8af1: "Pump or Tower Fan Status Mismatch Alarm" - Level 2 if on/off mismatch after 15s, Level 4 if off/on after 60s. Evidence supports. Accept.

cand_87d63de6dbd70616: "Tower Basin Heater Status Mismatch Alarm" - Level 2 if on/off after 15s, Level 4 if off/on after 15s. Evidence supports. Accept.

cand_459cfe5cca8ba1dd: "Piping Heat Trace Status Mismatch Alarm" - Similar. Accept.

cand_95760e79e515be34: "Two-Position Valve End Switch Alarm" - Level 2 if open but not open after 90s, Level 4 if closed but not closed after 90s. Accept.

cand_d1361f8facb69545: "Modulating Valve Position Feedback Alarm" - Level 2 if difference >10% for 90s. Accept.

cand_94c57a82f9929516: "Tower Basin Temperature Alarms" - Level 3 if <38°F for 5 min, Level 2 if <36°F for 5 min. Accept.

cand_b8f0c834c515b05a: "Sensor Failure Alarm" - Level 3 for monitoring, Level 2 for control. Accept.

cand_c21bef0cd3d3961c: "AFDD FC#1: DP Too High with Pumps Off" - Fault condition equation. Accept.

cand_084582bbe0c1cbfb: "AFDD FC#2: Primary Flow Too High with Pumps Off" - Accept.

cand_1a3b65cf62a5b5c9: "AFDD FC#4: DP Too Low with Pumps at Full Speed" - Accept.

cand_3a2c9a557ea5bff2: "AFDD FC#5: Primary Flow Too Low with Bypass Fully Open" - Accept.

cand_b3fa2aa6c2570f89: "AFDD FC#6: CHW Supply Temperature Too High" - Accept.

cand_018a0a8f493e7c7b: "AFDD FC#14: CWST Too High with Towers at Full Speed" - Accept.

Now, the ones with mismatched evidence: cand_9ed02d, cand_913d1f, cand_727f55, cand_4dad4d, cand_66bdb2, cand_a809a4, cand_442c3a, cand_e270aa. All of these have evidence quote that says: "If controlling cooling towers to maintain CWST (see 5.20.12.2 ), CWST sensor must be hardwired to the same" and source pages [39]. The summary does not match. These are likely unsupported by the provided evidence. So reject them.

Also, check for duplicates: no obvious duplicates.

Thus, for these, category_if_not: "unsupported".

For valid ones, we set is_valid_guideline36_knowledge to true, reason should be short, e.g., "Supported by evidence", and category_if_not: null.

For invalid ones, is_valid false, reason: "Evidence does not support the summary", category: "unsupported".

Now produce the JSON.
